spec.body:
# ZergStack

**Current rule, updated from Idan Slack feedback on 2026-05-14:** ZergStack / zstack is the infrastructure and runtime stack, not the umbrella brand for user-facing products.

Public brand name is **ZergStack** when referring to the infrastructure product or runtime layer. Lowercase `zstack` is reserved for URLs, filesystem paths, config, and internal shorthand. Older copy that uses "ZergStack" as the product-family umbrella should be treated as stale until rewritten.

Source of truth for managed apps: `~/zerg/zstack.yaml`. Internal control plane: `~/zerg/zstack/` (a mini Render/Railway that deploys to Fly.io and surfaces live status). The public framing is close to Render/Vercel style infrastructure: code push -> deploy, runtime, app hosting, service status, and shared primitives.

## Zerg Products

Default temporary umbrella language for user-facing products is **Zerg products**. Use this until Matt/Idan choose a final family term. Do not use "marketplace" as the default umbrella; it implies third-party contributors and payments before that model is decided.

Current user-facing products / product concepts:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergboard/Zergboard|Zergboard]] — project / task management (Trello/Linear-style) · competitive: [[Competitive/pm-software/index|pm-software]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCRM/ZergCRM|ZergCRM]] — internal-only, on roadmap, no early-access flow
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergChat/ZergChat|ZergChat]] — Slack-style team messaging · competitive: [[Competitive/internal-chat/index|internal-chat]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCal/ZergCal|ZergCal]] — calendar service (humans, interop, agent-native) · competitive: [[Competitive/calendar/index|calendar]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMeeting/ZergMeeting|ZergMeeting]] — meeting capture / notes / actioning · competitive: [[Competitive/video-meetings/index|video-meetings]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMail/ZergMail|ZergMail]] — agent-friendly email server · competitive: [[Competitive/workspace-email/index|workspace-email]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]] — wallet for zergs to hold/spend USD/BTC/ETH · competitive: [[Competitive/finance-and-wallets/index|finance-and-wallets]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergaudience/zergaudience-audience-taxonomy|Zergaudience]] — inbound capture / contacts / segments layer

Integration narrative: ZergMeeting → Zergboard → ZergChat → ZergCal → ZergMail. Full mapping (data flow, technical foundations, shipped-vs-spec'd status) lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Integration|Integration Narrative]].

Cross-product competitive sweeps should now say "Zerg products" unless the claim is specifically about the ZergStack infra layer.

## Apps Managed by ZergStack

These may be deployed via `zstack.yaml`, but that does not automatically make them part of a public product-family brand:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergBox/ZergBox|ZergBox]] — file/upload utility app
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZMsg/ZMsg|ZMsg]] — provider-aware messaging gateway (SMS/MMS/WhatsApp via Telnyx, Twilio)

The engineering-platform layer (`Zerg`, `ZDE`, `ZTC`, `Zerg Cloud`, `zterm`) is separate scope from both public Zerg products and the ZergStack infra page. Current internal evidence and open reliability/onboarding findings live in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]]; zterm product/backlog direction lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zterm/Zterm|Zterm]].

## Brand & Naming Conventions

- Public/long names: `Zergboard`, `ZergCRM`, `ZergChat`, `ZergCal`, `ZergMeeting`, `ZergMail`, `Zergwallet`, `Zergaudience`, `ZergStack`
- Short names reserved for internal/dev: `zboard`, `zchat`, `zcal`, `zmail`, `zcrm`
- One domain per product (registration tracked under WEB-30 on the Website Zergboard board)
- Product pages can say "runs on ZergStack" when discussing infra/runtime. They should not use "part of ZergStack" as the primary product positioning.
- Green-heavy product pages are not the default visual direction for Zergboard/Zergwallet. Per Idan 2026-05-14, use the more subdued Zergaudience-style dark treatment when using the green palette.

## Pricing

**Locked 2026-05-05** (vampire-attack on individual SaaS competitors):

| Tier | Price | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | Volume-capped per product |
| Basic | **$1/seat/mo** | Full feature surface; no agent governance |
| Pro | **$9/seat/mo** | + Scoped per-agent API tokens, tenant-safe routes, SSO, audit, advanced roles |
| Enterprise | Custom | + SLA, BYO-KMS, on-prem, SOC 2 / DPA |

Bundle SKUs remain proposed and should be renamed away from "Zstack" in public copy unless Matt/Idan explicitly choose that terminology. Working internal examples: **$4/seat Zerg products Basic** (20% off 5x Basic) and **$19/seat Zerg products Pro** (58% off 5x Pro). Strategy memory: `project_zstack_pricing.md`.

Per-product feature splits: [[Writing/Zstack Microproduct Pricing (WEB-31)]]. Category-leader benchmarks: [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Pricing-Snapshot|Pricing Snapshot]] (now updated with locked decisions). Implementation rolled out for Zergboard + zmail in PR #283.

## Auth & Security

- Auth unification deferred until product-family bundle launch (WEB-10); provider pre-work under WEB-38
- Security page lives at zergai.com/security; MNDA-gated request flow (no public PDFs)
- SOC 2 Type II target placeholder Q3 2026 (WEB-37)
- DPA / sub-processor list / MNDA template under WEB-34, WEB-35, WEB-36

## Bootstrap Recipe (New Microproduct)

From [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]]. The recipe generalizes to any new Zerg product:

1. `cd ~/zerg && mkdir <appname> && cd <appname>`
2. `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`
3. `/rename <appname>`
4. Paste a bootstrap prompt referencing `~/zerg/zergboard` and `~/zerg/zchat` as patterns to mimic (both use the ZTC embedded chat agent)
5. Use a branch like `zstack/<appname>` to keep work isolated

## Related

- [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] — board-level pull from the zergboard-skill
- [[Epoch/Projects/Product Glossary|Product Glossary]] — context on Zerg, ZDE, ZTC, ZStack as engineering layer
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]] — internal engineering-platform source of truth and Slack-sourced evidence capture

matrix.source_summary: landing/features pages for all 12 competitors explicitly list payroll processing
explicit multi-state payroll on Gusto, Rippling, Justworks, BambooHR, Paylocity landing/features pages
Deel, Rippling, Workday, ADP, Remote, TriNet, Paychex confirm global payroll; Gusto/Justworks limited to contractors/EOR
Gusto (unlimited payrolls, 4-day/next-day/same-day/instant pay), Rippling (off-cycle payroll), Justworks (off-cycle payments) explicitly list
explicit contractor payments/management on Gusto, Rippling, Justworks, Deel, BambooHR, ADP, Remote
EOR explicitly listed for Gusto, Rippling, Justworks, Deel, BambooHR, Remote
Rippling and Remote landing/features explicitly list contractor of record
Justworks, Rippling, ADP, Paychex, Remote, TriNet explicitly market PEO services
explicit tax filing/tax compliance on Gusto, Justworks, BambooHR, ADP, Paylocity, HiBob, Paychex, TriNet
Gusto (1099 filings) and Justworks (w-2 and 1099 filings) explicitly list; others imply via 'tax filing' but not confirmed
ADP (wage garnishment) and Paylocity (garnishment management) explicitly list
only Gusto explicitly lists direct deposit; standard payroll feature but unconfirmed on others
Gusto (instant pay, Gusto Wallet) and Paylocity (payroll on-demand/earned wage access) explicitly list
ADP explicitly lists prepaid pay card; no others confirm
Deel explicitly lists multi-currency support; others likely have via global payroll but unconfirmed
explicit 'multiple pay rates' in Gusto features, 'multi-rate pay' in BambooHR, and Justworks lists 'multiple pay rates'
explicit 'benefits administration' listed in features across Rippling, BambooHR, ADP, Paylocity, HiBob, Paychex, TriNet
explicit 'health insurance' in Gusto, ADP (group health insurance), Paychex, Justworks (medical insurance); TriNet employee benefits admin
explicit '401k retirement' in Gusto, '401k' in Justworks, 'retirement plans' in ADP, '401k and retirement' in Paychex
explicit 'workers compensation' in Gusto, Justworks ('workers comp'), ADP ('workers compensation insurance'), Paychex
explicit 'employment practices liability insurance' in Justworks and Paychex features
'state unemployment insurance administration' in Justworks, 'state unemployment insurance' in Paychex, 'unemployment claims' in ADP
explicit 'cobra administration' only observed in Justworks features
explicit 'hsa fsa' only observed in Justworks features
explicit 'commuter benefits' only observed in Justworks features
explicit 'time and attendance' listed in features across Rippling, ADP, Paylocity, Paychex, TriNet, BambooHR, HiBob
explicit 'scheduling' in Gusto, Rippling, BambooHR features
explicit 'time off management' in Deel, HiBob, BambooHR ('time-off management'), Justworks ('pto management'), Gusto ('pto policies/time off requests')
explicit 'leave management' in TriNet features; others show time-off but not distinct leave management
'employee records' explicit in BambooHR; 'hris'/'core hr system of record' in Rippling, Deel, HiBob, Remote, Workday; 'employee self-service portal' in Paylocity
explicit org chart/directory features in Justworks, Deel, BambooHR, Rippling HRIS, HiBob core HR
HiBob esign/document management, Paylocity document management, Justworks document center, Deel contract lifecycle
explicit ATS in BambooHR, Paylocity, ADP, Paychex; Remote Recruit; Rippling recruiting
onboarding explicitly listed across nearly all sources' feature lists
explicit offboarding in Rippling (90-sec offboarding), Justworks, BambooHR (clear terminated employee data), HiBob
Paychex and Remote list background checks as native; BambooHR as feature; others via Checkr integration
performance management/reviews explicit in Gusto, Rippling, BambooHR, Paylocity, HiBob, ADP, Workday
explicit LMS in Rippling, Paylocity, HiBob, Paychex, Workday (talent mgmt)
compensation management explicitly listed in Gusto, BambooHR, Paylocity, HiBob, Workday (incentive comp mgmt)
BambooHR benchmarking data, Paylocity peer benchmarking, HiBob Mercer-powered comp benchmarking
HiBob explicit skills management; Workday talent management includes skills
Remote explicit work visas & permits; Rippling/Deel via EOR infrastructure
Remote explicit stock options administration; others via Carta integration only

positioning.elevator_pitch: ZergStack HRIS-Payroll is an agent-native people ops backbone for AI-first companies that runs payroll, benefits, and hiring workflows without the enterprise sales cycle or the seat-tax roulette.
core_differentiator_lines: Agent-native HRIS: your zergs are first-class employees
Flat, published pricing — no 'contact sales' shell game
Integrated with Zergboard, ZergCal, ZergMail out of the box
One control plane for humans, contractors, and agent workloads
Deploy your people ops the way you deploy code

headline_candidates.headline: People ops for companies that ship like software.
headline_candidates.subhead: Payroll, benefits, and hiring in one agent-native stack — priced like infrastructure, not enterprise HR.

headline_candidates.headline: Your HRIS shouldn't need an implementation team.
headline_candidates.subhead: Skip the six-week Paylocity rollout. ZergStack HR is live the day you sign up.

headline_candidates.headline: Public prices. No demo required.
headline_candidates.subhead: Every tier, every add-on, on the pricing page — the way Rippling and Workday won't do it.

headline_candidates.headline: The only HRIS that treats your AI agents as headcount.
headline_candidates.subhead: Manage humans and zergs in one directory, one permissions model, one audit log.

headline_candidates.headline: HRIS that lives inside the same stack as your work.
headline_candidates.subhead: Org chart, tasks, calendar, mail, and payroll — one identity graph, no Zapier duct tape.

messaging_pillars.promise: Humans and AI agents share one directory, permissions model, and audit trail.
proof_points: Zergs appear in the org chart with role, owner, and cost center
Same RBAC governs human PII and agent secrets
Payroll for humans + spend ledger for agents in one report

messaging_pillars.promise: Flat, published per-seat pricing with no 'call for a quote' opacity.
proof_points: Full price grid on the marketing site
No mandatory annual commit to see numbers
No hidden per-module upsells for reporting or SSO

messaging_pillars.pillar: Live the day you sign up
messaging_pillars.promise: Onboard in an afternoon, not a quarter — no implementation partner required.
proof_points: Self-serve payroll dry-run before first live run

messaging_pillars.promise: HRIS is wired natively into Zergboard, ZergCal, ZergMail, ZergChat, and Zergwallet.
proof_points: New hire gets task board, calendar, mail, and wallet provisioned in one flow
Termination revokes access across every Zerg product atomically
Single audit log across work, comms, and payroll

messaging_pillars.promise: Real humans in-hours, not a ticket queue that goes cold post-implementation.
proof_points: Published response-time SLA on public site
Same team pre-sale and post-sale — no handoff to a slower support tier
No 'premium support' upsell to get a human

anti_pitch: Not an enterprise suite: we won't ship 53 modules of half-mature functionality the way Workday does
Not contact-sales-only: we won't hide pricing behind a demo funnel like Rippling, Workday, and Deel
Not a global-payroll EOR aggregator on day one: we won't overpromise 150-country coverage like Deel/Remote before we can deliver it
Not a 6-week implementation product: we won't require a paid onboarding SKU like Paylocity
Not an à-la-carte upsell maze: reporting, SSO, and API access aren't premium add-ons
Not a PEO by default: we don't force co-employment like Justworks unless the customer explicitly opts in
positioning.icp_refinement: AI-native and dev-tools startups, 5–150 headcount, US-domestic payroll first, that already run their business on modern infra (Fly/Vercel/Render/Linear/Slack) and want their HRIS to feel like the rest of their stack — priced flat, self-serve, API-first, and aware that a meaningful share of their 'workforce' is agents, not humans. Secondary ICP: agencies and small consultancies who need contractor + employee payments in one system without Deel-tier global complexity.

objection_handling.objection: Rippling / Gusto is more mature and has every integration
objection_handling.response: True — and you'll pay for 40 modules to use 6. We cover the table-stakes HRIS + payroll surface, publish our roadmap, and integrate natively with the Zerg products you're already adopting. Maturity in modules you don't use isn't a feature.

objection_handling.objection: Deel / Remote cover 150+ countries for global payroll
objection_handling.response: We're US-domestic + contractor-global on day one and honest about it. If you need EOR in 40 countries today, we're not your fit yet — but most 20-person AI startups don't, and Deel's global surface comes with Deel's pricing and sales motion.

objection_handling.objection: Justworks bundles benefits via PEO and it's simpler
objection_handling.response: PEO means co-employment and giving up your own EIN and benefits control. We give you the same simplicity without locking you into a co-employer relationship you'll have to unwind at Series B.

objection_handling.objection: Paylocity / ADP are the safe enterprise choice
objection_handling.response: Safe means 6-week implementations, slow post-sale support, and reporting workarounds — all documented complaints. We optimize for the opposite: same-day live, self-serve reporting, one support tier.

objection_handling.objection: You're new — what if you disappear?
objection_handling.response: Full data export in standard payroll/HRIS schemas, no proprietary lock-in, and every integration runs on the same public APIs we ship for customers. Migration in is one afternoon; migration out is the same.

objection_handling.objection: Agent-native sounds like a gimmick — we just need payroll
objection_handling.response: Fine — run it as a straight HRIS + payroll and ignore the agent features. The moment you add your first production AI agent to a workflow, you'll want it in the same directory as the humans it works with.
